channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot update — 34 replies — 79.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:36 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399251
Slurped

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

62401739
>>62401689
Interesting
Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877
>>62401739
A lambo will be in your future

62401946
>>62401877
Checked.

62402307
>>62399251
You have a missed call

62402326
>>62402317
Didn't NEO have a similar issue

62402419
>>62402317
it's just an nft with price discovery so they don't have to do all of that coretime auction bullshit again

62403350
>>62402317

Thats not even what its use is for

62403448
>>62402317

im surprised dot isnt trading at 10 dollars considering the pedigree

but they overspent on marketing during a bear market instead of 2021. but on the plus side the dot treasury has started reporting quarterly profits. Unlike Cardano or Chainlink

it wont stay under a dollar for long

62404229
>>62403448
>it wont stay under a dollar for long

Under 50 cents by 2027

62404438
0.87 new low lmao. god what a piece of shit.

62404588
15k @ 7$.
I dont care

62404599
>>62404229
It'll go to 0. Nobody cares; it's a useless chain that serves to inflate Gayvin's ego, making him believe he's intelligent and respected. This desperate propaganda on /biz/ shows how dead this project is.

62405376
>>62401877
Checked
I've heard that before
We'll see
Been waiting for an actual parallel economy system for fucking ever
Was watching komodo and loki/oxen before this and they never got off the ground

62405404
Depression

62405433
Didn't read but is he hugging DOT and shilling a new token?

62406887
I bought more

62408015
>>62406887
Rookie move

62408521
>>62404588

If you buy 15k more your average will be 3.50

Why not DCA?

62408874
>>62404438

.885

62411243
wowie

62413316
>>62405433

JAM will not be a new token but will be an on chain platform with Polkadotbl

item.title: >see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires" >leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors wh… — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires" >leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors wh…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:52 UTC

OP

>see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires"
>leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors who had 40+ years of compounding
>bought blue chips when they were practically giving them away
>fast forward to today
>if you're under 50 the percentage is probably way lower
>it id 0.2%
>that is less than 1 in 40 oma
>look around the next time you're in a grocery store
>9.9 out of 10 people aren't sitting on $1M in liquid investments
>the guy with seven figures isn't standing in line with you
>everyone else is arguing over 5% raises and coupon apps
>the wealth gap isn't a gap
>it's a different universe


Why will YOU be in the top 0.2%?

Early replies (first 3)

62412965
>>62412950
Because I hate niggers and jews, but I'm good at playing the jew-game. God has chosen me to be the chosen one, the one who will fund antisemite institutions and finally make the final solution real, as the Lord sad it will once come to play with the real Messiah. Amen.

62413039
>>62412950
I thought the same way when I was 20. But I invested anyway, and now I'm one of the liquid millionaires with $2.6m liquid. I stand in line at the grocery store like everybody else. If you want to become rich then sitting around on your ass bitching about how unfair life is because nobody is handing you million dollar checks isn't the way to do it.

62413053
>>62412950
>bought blue chips when they were practically giving them away
Actually at the time I was pissed at how expensive they were. You want to buy blue chips then hop to it asap, they have a tendency to get more expensive over time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413065
>>62412950
I’m 35 and my NW is 140k.

Will booglehead tier investing get me to “a little better than average” if I’m dumping 2kish a month in my brokerage and maxing my Roth IRA yearly?

62413067
>>62412950
The wealth gap is natural.

62413069
>>62412950

Just buy DOT lol

https://polkadot.cloud/blog/en/jam-12-to-20-months-m1

62413075
>>62413039
Not really, you got lucky. 2.5% of people have 1m at best. How old are you now?
>>62413039
>>62413053
Income is getting fucked now so people don’t have much to invest. It is savings first then investments. People don’t have savings.

62413088
I'm not going to sit in a million bucks worth of SGOV just to say I'm a liquid millionaire.

62413103
>>62413088
1m net worth is still 10% of Americans and most of it is tied up in primary home.

62413143
>>62413075
I got lucky by investing instead of sitting around whining about not being rich and gaslighting myself into thinking it was impossible. Stop being such a useless whiny little bitch and work on improving your finances

62413167
>>62413143
But you’re old now.

62413200
>>62413167
Nope. I just put in the work to make myself wealthy, carefully evaluating stocks and taking risks that I thought were reasonable. Nobody is going to walk by and just toss you a couple mil just for the lulz. If you want to become rich, it's up to you to get yourself there.

62413214
>>62413200
Yeah you got lucky lol.

62413325
>>62413214
Working hard and investing intelligently isn't luck no matter how many times you say it. If you want to sit on your ass and whine like a bitch and be poor, then have at it. But don't lie to me and everybody else about how "unlucky" you are while you're at it .

62413354
>>62412950
Having passive money is like playing on god mode

62413357
>>62413325
how old are you? can you comprehend basic questions?

62413365
>>62413357
Ask more politely next time.

62413388
>>62413365
You’re stupid and got lucky. The stats show that.

item.title: Double bottom is in, AI money is rotating in. Massive 2018 signals — 26 replies — 53.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Double bottom is in, AI money is rotating in. Massive 2018 signals

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:33 UTC

OP

Don't listen to anons look at facts. This is the only coin currently being aggressively accumulated on a weekly basis (BMNR/SBET). This is a classic Wyckoff structure, they're accumulating while retail capitulates.
No other coin has the momentum of ETH at the moment. RWAs/billionaire interest/BTC generational exits especially due to quantum computing.

Early replies (first 3)

62406476
This. The flippening is inevitable.

62406502
Muh efferiums

62406529
OP here, accidentally posted the wrong image

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62406530
>>62406441
I don't know anon I'm so demoralized. Been holding this shit since 2019 and last cycle has been so disappointing with ETH barely making a new high. and the ETH/BTC chart looks like complete shit

62406655
>>62406441
>This is the only coin currently being aggressively accumulated on a weekly basis (BMNR/SBET)
This means nothing. Like nothing at all. The only hope to save ETH right now is ETHLab. Ethereum Foundation has gone fucking insane. I have never been more bearish on Ethereum.

62406696
>>62406441
Yeah you dont sound like a desperate bagholder at all

62407072
>>62406441
1500 is back on the menu bois!

62407099
>>62406655
You must be retarded, stop posting. BMNR accumulation is highly bullish. They're going to shill to ETF bros, the play has ALWAYS been that crypto comes back by November 2026 (midterms). Wait til the trump family shill it to the masses again.

62407114
>>62406441
>while retail capitulates

That alone takes minimum 6-12 months and it's only just started

62407136
>>62407114
Don't confuse ETH with BTC, ETH is certainly coming out of its bear. Always ends with a double bottom right about now in the cycle.

62407160
>>62407136
>Always ends with a double bottom right about now in the cycle

This is uncharted territory, bitcoin hash rate is crashing since all the farms are converting to AI, people are super bullish on stocks and not fans of crypto, this isn't 2018 and 2022 anymore

62407201
>>62407160
Be fair about this and admit this is exactly what people said in 2023. Truth is what has value doesn't really matter, in this market rhetorics and memes are in the driver's seat.
Elites are controlling these market movements, not what you think. It's all about what they can trick retail into buying. Crypto will continue to be cyclical and no one will ever understand why, but retail will continue to misunderstand this. It's every four years and it's basically scripted.

BTC is in trouble from QC. ETH is going to win this in the end.

62407204
>>62407160
Hash rate doesnt matter, difficulty will adjust

62407511
>>62406441
>dumps hard instead
lmao can't make this shit up

62407533
>>62406441
The money can't leave AI. It's a financial black hole. It will just consume more and more of whatever liquidity is left and cause everything else to crash.

62407622
>>62407201
>BTC is in trouble from QC. ETH is going to win this in the end.

For Eth to be #1, bitcoin will have to go to sub-1,000 dollars, which would cause a multi-year crash

>>62407204
Transaction fee crisis like 2018

62407633
>>62406441
Not yet, but soon.
When companies to tell employees to stop tokenmaxxing, then it's here.

62407797
>>62407160
>all the farms are converting to AI,
Is this a new fud? Do you honestly believe miners throw away their bitcoin ASICs to buy overpriced GPUs so they could... do what? Run Kimi on them? Rent them to OpenAI?
Many miners are also located near power plants and utilize occasional excess power. They will lose this advantage if they move to AI computes which needs steady and stable availability.

62407910
>>62407797
fucking newfags never saw a miner....

62408040
>>62407204
Is this the new Bitcoin maxi cope? Will the next one be "51% attack isn't actually a bad thing"?

62408089
>>62408040
Im not a maxi and its very very far from 51% being feasible

62409110
>>62407099
Nigger november is most likely when they let the stock market reset gigacrash which will most likely take bitcoin with it.
Shit is fucked. Stock market at all time high and bitcoin is dumping before the megadump is even gonna happen in november.
I still have all but the altcoins I’m most bullish on long term just in case but I exited everything else.

62409256
all the leaders of eth just left the foundation. shit is over. sell while you can

62410273
>>62406441
>Double bottom is in, AI money is rotating in. Massive 2018 signals
wrong on all counts.
its going to 3 digits first, and then to zero.

62410343
>>62410273
>wrong on all counts.
>its going to 3 digits first, and then to zero.

half correct
yes it will go to 3 digits
but not to zero, most unlikely. recover maybe in the year 2030 or later...

62413299
>>62406476
yes. ETH will be flipped by tether.

item.title: Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150? — 11 replies — 35.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:43 UTC

OP

Why does SPCX have such a hard floor at $150? Every time it gets under $151, like clockwork, someone buys

Early replies (first 3)

62412944
>>62412935
This has always been 100% a Man.

62413007
>>62412935
The person on this picture is transgender right? It has a penis right?

62413061
>>62412935
because vested interests require $150 to prop up a valuation of $1.5 trillion on a mountain of absolute fucking garbage.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413074
>>62412944
>>62413007
No. Don't feel bad, many people have thought the same thing. Its not your fault tranners have gotten so hot over the past decade nce.

62413079
>>62412944
That man has the most fuckable face on internet.

62413083
>>62413074
This kid was like 2018

62413094
>>62412935
Those are the limit orders that didn't go through 2 weeks ago

62413098
>>62413094
Interesting, could you tell me more about that? So there are people who placed an order at $150 when it IPOd and then just left them there?

62413107
>>62413007
Uses her finger to penetrate

62413251
HIT OR MISS
I GUESS THEY NEVER MISS HUH?

62413351
>>62413098
>So there are people who placed an order at $150 when it IPOd and then just left them there?
Yes.

thats someones son

This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in cry…

OP

This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto. Not a single goddamn one. ZERO exceptions

Early replies (first 3)

62410760
i mean he looks like a sojack, thats kind of on you

62413165
This, combined with fallout from the LINK AMA, is funny as hell. Assblaster was a fraud

62413241
>>62410743
Give context you nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413259
>>62413241

>Assblaster was a fraud

62413262

https://www.coinmetro.com/blog/announcement-onboarding-depositing-and-withdrawals

item.title: it's fucking over — 7 replies — 6.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
it's fucking over

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:51 UTC

OP

the market just closed with BTC under $60k.

any fund with any significant position still left in MSTR is not going to want to hold this shit over the weekend when the current setup is for an ultraviolent dump down to $20k.

especially not the weekend before the end of Q2 that puts their entire Q2/H1 numbers they report to investors at risk.

every analyst right now is going to be scrambling to figure out how they can offload this shit before end of day friday or profit off the collapse if they don't already have a short position.

>inb4 bitcoin trades 24/7 market hours don't matter
the entire 2025 bull thesis was predicated on ETFs and institutions buying BTC, they did, and it's the only thing that matters now

Early replies (first 3)

62412892
>>62412870
You hear that?

62413038
>>62412870
btc is part of boomer funds now. it could go lower but it's hard to do so because a significant percentage of holdings are now sitting untouched in boomer retirement accounts. lower moves trigger mass autobuys by these funds. retail shorts will get liquidated instantly.

62413046
>>62412870

This FUD is a golden buy signal

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413095
>>62412870
fyi

https://www.tradingview.com/news/gurufocus:dab44ae21094b:0-10-billion-bitcoin-options-expiry-puts-market-under-fresh-pressure/

Let's see how this will unwind tomorrow. Claude said this could put a bearish pressure.

62413100
>>62413038
>holdings are now sitting untouched

ETF's and stocks aren't bitcoin

62413111
>>62412870
>the market just closed
??????????????????????????

62413116
>>62413100
ETF's and stocks that hold the underlying which in this case is Bitcoin are purchased en masse by financial institutions as they are legally obligated to do so. Not doing so would be securities fraud and they'd be subject to sec violations.

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 62 replies — 72.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:10 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62404304
>why aren't you holding goy gold
/biz/ is already out of crypto.
https://web.archive.org/web/202602162103...f-bitcoin/

62404487
>>62404304
Lol

62404614
>>62404122
Why do you need fiat?

62405784
>>62401190
Why though? If you can earn from your BTC while maintaining self-custody, I don't see the problem.

62405818
>>62398819
If it's still BTC and I still control the keys, I don't see why it stops being Bitcoin.

62406014
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
I will buy when it's cheaper. 100 bucks seems like a fair value for the future of finance.

62406579
>>62399676
I guess not but..

62406783
There is simply no reason for it to have any value boss.

62409051
>>62399898
You are a fucking retard. The current price means NOTHING. Its NOTHING! Lower the better to buy more.
Boomers are still in the seats of power. They dont embrace BTC because most boomers not in some kind of power see it as not something they can walk into a bank and ask their financial advisor to get. Therefore its fake and gay to them.
The boomers being the biggest voting block, are the most catered to. So basically the reason why the world has not been embracing digital is because of these fucking idiots.
Once boomers are out of the seats of power and fucking gone and millennials are in charge its going to get real digital.BTC will inevitably be embraced.
More countries will just adopt it as their main currency like El Salvador. Its the best logical option. Your economy is crumbling and the nations dollar is shit? Just adopt bitcoin its quite simple.

Not only that, but owning a Bitcoin will be so rare, everything will cost in Satoshis. Once Amazon adopts a bitcoin logo payment option its 10000% on. Once Musk post some stupid twitter picture of a moon bitcoin mining farm its going to moon.

Get it now while its cheap. Price has no meaning at the moment. If your a day trader I get why you may be pessimistic about it. But if your a hodler and believe in BTC Philosophically and not thinking of it like a share/stock then thats the right idea.

BTC is known by everyone. If you went to what ever hood you are in and asked the most wet cigarette smelling schizo hobo " Hey have you heard of that digital gold thing" the response would be some schizo babble with "That bitcoin stuff:"

Its backed by million computers. It takes IRL power/electrical energy and time (mining) to produce. Thats what money realy is or better to say what gives something value. Time+Energy. Its basically electricity converted into money. And electricity is not free.

HODL is the right idea for a boomerless digital embraced future. dont let Day Traders do it for quick buck not future.

62409106
>>62395695
>"those companies work for your money."
>babby's first dable on stocks
>doesn't know owners are diluting your investment and buying mansions with the ATM

62409107
>>62399898
To add to this, You are thinking of like 10x+ full BTC holders. Average persons goal is to have a single one or 2 to just hold on to the future.
The scenario you are talking about is like for some kind of rich guy living on the lamb which is like 1 out of 10 million people.

The real persons goal is to get a bitcoin and maybe it will be like 300,000 per coin in the future. Then you have enough for your familys college or whatever. Just a good foundation for a son/daughters future. Not somthing you just cash out to cartoonishly and buy lambos with and post videos of your garage with hexagon light ceilings.

62409236
>>62405784
You know it's a risky move.

62409560
>>62409236
Depends what you mean by risky. I wouldn't touch an unaudited protocol, but I also don't think every new protocol carries the same level of risk.

62409564
>>62399898
Governments taxing something doesn't make it dead. If anything, it usually means they've accepted it's an asset class that's here to stay.

62409803
>>62409560
More like defi hacks

62409813
>>62409107
It's possible 10 years from now. Pass it to your kids

62409997
>>62409803
That's why I'm watching Babylon instead of blindly aping into random protocols. They've been taking audits and testing seriously.

62410003
>>62406014
People have gone broke waiting for the perfect entry just as often as they've bought too early.

62410039
>>62410003
So what's the best time to buy

62410525
>>62410039
everytime, imo.

62411160
>>62410525
I don't have money

62411213
>>62409997
I think their TBVs could unlock a lot of Bitcoin use cases that weren't really possible before. We'll know a lot more once the Aave V4 public testnet is complete tho.

62411224
>>62409051
Pls summary

62411241
>>62396002
What About gold and silver?

62411256
>>62404304
I'm not convinced .

62411265
I will just keep my 0.3 btc somewhere

62411638
>>62395915
Institutions are getting onboard, It might do 10X in a decade, also lending is starting to become more relevant with the likes of TBV, there are so many utilities untapped.

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

62413082
>>62411638
That's the part most people miss. BTC doesn't have to just sit in a wallet forever.

62413193
>>62406783
YOU'RE RETARD

item.title: LET'S FUCKING GOOOOOOOOO — 17 replies — 48.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
LET'S FUCKING GOOOOOOOOO

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62411656

62411659
>>62411646
many riches coming to your way soon saar

62411665
Greed ID you'll wagmi it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62411719
>>62411646
How did you even get that much money together?

62412778
>>62411646
Too early, 55k eom.

62412802
40K October 2026

62412858
>>62411646

62412867
>>62412858
you lost so much fucking money you stupid cuckold

62412871
>>62412867
oh shit i struck a nerve you're seething

62412911
>>62411646
Satoshi Kings it is our time.

62412927
>>62412867
Link really does live rent free in your head

62413228
>>62411646
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=utLgqCiwwZ8

62413272
>>62412858
People like you are the reason why I am still here. God be with you.

62413278
>>62413272
>People like you are the reason why I am still here.
What did you mean by this?

>God be with you.
Thanks I've been swing trading my SOL. Brought it up from around ~600 to 750 so far. Trying to get to $1000.

62413283
>>62411646

Hell yeah

62413337
>>62413278
>What did you mean by this?
People with nerve and patience. Beyond the memes I hope you make it

62413342
>>62413337

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 158 replies — 43.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 158 • Updated: 2026-06-25 18:25 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398868
>>62398799
You are better off getting a job

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314

item.title: ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH… — 67 replies — 20.1/hr

62406483

62406488
>>62406474

so called "bagholding" was the plan all along!!

62408348

Just in time for me to start my bulk :')

62408419

kek cryptards are getting brain damage from being fucked so hard

62408483

62408988
>>62406449

62409027
>>62406449

62409062
They bought silver because a rich indian proved its a scam,,,,,,lmao,,,,,,pol brain and investing is a deadly combination

62409141

62409263
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SO BULLISH AT SILVER

62409270
>>62409263

62409295
>>62409263

Comex and Shanghai exchange inventories have been going up now for a solid month. Industry is thrifting silver and replacing it as much as possible with copper and in silver just being a rock it has no economic value really compared to gold.

62409326

62409335

62409381

62409428

62409431

62409935
>>62409428

>silver just dumps

62409962
>>62409428
Silver and gold dump dring a crisis loljust look at how they dumped harder than the stock market during the financial crisis.

62410278

62410350
>>62406501
so why not just donate the money to charity then if you can never sell ?

62410373
>>62409326

t.stacktard

62410390

62410451
Bought a monster box in 2019 and another 200 oz over 2020-2021

Still up 3x

62410696
>>62410451

>Still up 3x

lol

62410726

TO DA MOOOOOOON!

62410762

62411945
>>62410762

62412080
>>62407084

62412276

item.title: Who uses Polkadot? — 7 replies — 16.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who uses Polkadot?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-25 21:37 UTC

OP

With the Cardano dApp ecosystem completely imploding from the TapTools closure and the SecondFi exploit, it’s completely obvious Substrate is the only architectural survivor.

Cardano-cels thought Haskell was safe, but asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets. Now that the DOT treasury is finally pivoting away from multi-million dollar logo rebranding and actually funding core developer infrastructure, the ecosystem metrics are completely undeniable.

Why aren't you loading up on DOT at these prices before the parachain coretime marketplace goes fully liquid? It's literally a mathematically guaranteed breakout.

Early replies (first 3)

62412459
>>62412453

I do use Polkadot everyday

Its on my autobuy

62412625
>>62412453
awaiting the next bridge exploit to completely drain the polkadot system yet again

62412713
>>62412625

This is impossible

Polkadot is one of the most secure cryptos

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412947
>>62412713
look up what happened to acala and moonbeam, the biggest parachains

62412986
>>62412947

1. Aftermath: Acala recovered relatively well with community/governance intervention. This remains the main notable incident for the project. No major new breaches reported since.

2. No direct security breach: Searches turn up no evidence of a core protocol hack or exploit on Moonbeam itself. It was tangentially involved in the Acala incident (e.g., as a destination for some swapped funds or via shared Polkadot ecosystem elements like XCM channels)

???

62413181
>>62412986
AI is such shit. Acala never fucking recovered, it has a marketcap under 1 mil and legitimately went to zero (picrel). At the same time you had Moonbeam get completely drained by the Nomad bridge hack, with the team insisting they did no wrong despite being responsible for selecting the bridge against the protests of their own community and the bridge being financially backed by people affiliated with the team.

62413320
Nobody cares dude. Security? Scalability? Who gives a shit, all crypto is heading to zero. Agile Coretime and asynchronous who-gives-a-fuck… do you still think it’s 2021? I don’t care how agile the coretime is, it’s all garbage. The sooner you realize that the better off you’ll be.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 75 replies — 16.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 75 • Updated: 2026-06-25 17:30 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62405252
>>62405201
Are you replying to the wrong post all that guy did was ask questions

62405400
>>62404617
they predicted a 5-6% YTD gain for 2026 and we're already above that, lying shits.

62406764
>>62400493
Rather buy VT. Not so bullish on USA anymore

62406776
>>62406764
Still, conceptually it’s sound right?

62406804
>>62400493
>QQQ
if you want more returns (with more risk), you should do something like leverage + vt (or similar) instead of voo (you should at least do vti) and global small cap value, not sector etfs

62406811
>>62406804
in English doc

62406843
>>62406811
Alright! I'm gonna explain it like you're 5 years old!

Think of investing like choosing a basket of seeds: VOO is like only planting the biggest U.S. trees, VTI is like planting the whole U.S. garden, VT is like planting the whole world garden, and global small cap value is like adding lots of tiny, scrappy plants that have sometimes grown faster over long periods; if you want more possible growth and can stomach bigger ups and downs, using leverage is like turning up the volume on a diversified basket, which can help more than just buying one loud sector ETF, because sectors are a bet on one part of the economy and can stay out of favor for a long time, while broad diversification plus leverage is usually a cleaner way to aim for higher returns without relying on one slice of the market.

62406996
>>62406804
I am a noob and leverage seems scary desu like I will owe that money eventually right

62407040
>>62406996
yes, I'm not into leverage either, it evolves its own risks, but if you are not risk averse, it's better than gambling/prediction markts/crypto etc.

62407059
>>62400493
Voo used to pay more dividends before it was all fag7. Its basically the same as the nasdaq for the past decade

62407078
>>62400493
yeah I have like almost half a million in VTI right now, and I just need to sit on it for another 10 years. Its funny, I get excited whenever VTI goes down because it means i get a little bonus next deposit

62407086
Man I want to just have more principal to invest rather than chasing returns
Anyone know of good side hustles or AI grifts anyone can do
Idc as long as it’s triple digits a month at least

62407097
>>62406776
with Trump's economy + trade wars, VXUS actually went up a ton during the period and now a lot of countries are getting less and less dependent on America. If/when the AI bubble ever pops then you're going to be a lot more insulated since the American economy is being propped up by it.

62409216
>>62407097
Makes sense but aren’t all global economies just going to be downstream of American AI / Computing companies turning a profit?

62409221
>>62402005
pr34ch

62409463
>>62400515
>t. down 95% in fartniggercoin

62409479
>ETF and chill is smart but I'm smarter I can time the market!

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today

item.title: Why do side hustles suck so fucking much? — 23 replies — 15.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do side hustles suck so fucking much?

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-06-25 20:32 UTC

OP

ALL of them are either
>Guaranteed to work below minimum wage
Or
>1% chance to have a good source of additional income but only after you put thousands of hours and years into it
Like why not just work overtime or request additional hours? Seems infinitely more reliable and more worth your time

Early replies (first 3)

62409369
>>62409347
l3l I was just thinking about this speedrun yesterday for some unknown reason. Stay out of my fucking head you nasty pervert.

62409375
>>62409347
Guy on the left is still autistic and the guy on the right is a woman now

62409383
>>62409347
The whole concept of a side hustle is a jewish scam to convince retards to work for pennies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409391
>>62409347
Side hustles are dumb. Just work at an hospital in the kitchen or in maintenance. Pay's pretty decent and the schedule is mad flexible

62409399
i think side hustles are for people whose jobs are strictly 9-5 mon-friday and they have no ability to take up more shifts


like for example, i know some health care workers who take as many shifts as physically possible, so they can make as much money as possible, where as some of them will only take a couple shifts per week

You will make more money doing ur same job for more hours than u ever will with a side hustle in 99% of cases, but ur job has to allow u to work more.

62409421
>>62409347
This thread looks fun

62409426
>>62409347
>side hustles
You're basically scraping the dingleberries off capitalism's hairy asscrack

62409443
You're better off maxxing your primary career and investing more

62409517
the main reason to not work more hours is the more you work someplace the worse you get treated. familiarity breeds contempt. so you work somewhere else so your boss doesn't see you so much and find something wrong with you that they will nag you about every day for the rest of your life

62409626
I love my side hustle

I get flown out to the Olympic games or soccer world cups every few years to do some technical work for okay money (15k$ / month) and then go back to my boring ass fulltime job. Just came back from Houston, Texas. World Cup.

62409628
Because it's good social media clickbait. The only ones making serious money mouthing off about side hustles are the content creators like Ramsey Solutions, etc.

62409743
Some side hustles are great, Anon. I had a side business fixing small engines in college off of a Facebook marketplace post. Made GREAT money and as a side benefit I always had a toy in the garage I got on a deal I could flex in the bros with.

Stop bitching on the internet and try harder.

62409748
>>62409626
I really doubt professional footballers would pay some /biz/ poster to suck their dick a few times for $15k only for you to go back to the trailer park you live in outside of Houston blowing guys for 50 bucks a pop.

62409755
>>62409383
I agree that's why they gave it a "cool name"

62409774
the idea that you can make more money while not working full time trying to weasle into someone else's turf who works full time is retarded, no wonder it's so popular among normies

62409878
>>62409748
desu it kinda makes me proud that what is literally my normals life sounds so outlandish to you that you can't even fathom someone doing these things

Literally have made it

62409946
>>62409347
>only after you put thousands of hours and years into it
Do you think it’s any different for literally everything in life? lol

By the way, working 8 hours per day every day for one year on your side hustle = almost 3,000 hours of work already

>Like why not just work overtime or request additional hours?
Ooooh please master give me extra little crumbs of your big cake, I’ve been such a good boy lately, I don’t deserve any freedom or respect but I might deserve these crumbs

62409951
im NEET so everythig i do is a sidehustle. its ok becuase wagecucks subsidize me through gibbs like free internet, discounted utilities, $850 in foodstamps every month, and 100% free health insurance. their quality of life sucks, mine is the same but im not running around with 220 hg bloodpressure every morning and afternoon clawing my way through masses of brown invaders and fellow wagecucks to do performative menial labor for mr shekelburg while making sure to fake socialize with office politics to not to give the HR roastie the ick and get me fired

62412436
>>62409383
>>62409399
>>62409426
>>62409443
this

>>62409743
>I had
>in college
It can be good temporarily if you're in the right place, right time, but long term it's shit and the time would be better spent building a real career.

62412455
>>62409347
>Why do side hustles suck so fucking much?
literally because if they didn't suck they'd be main careers.

>what's your side hustle?
>I'm a freelance pediatric neurosurgeon on weekends

If a side hustle pays good people are just going to do it full time and it's not a side hustle anymore. By definition side hustles have to suck.

62412543
>>62409347
My side hustle of scraping off fomo stocks every week or so have been going well. So has my pokemon card empire.

Must be a skill issue.

62412899
>>62409626
Whats your side hustle? That's my dream job

62412991
>>62409878
You're actually a high class gay escort for pro athletes?
Well shit man, I hope you're parlaying that income into some good instruments.
How did you even get into doing that?

>>62409946
C'mon, overtime can make some good money especially for someone who has lots of time and lack of nepo connections.

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 144 replies — 15.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-06-25 12:38 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62401552
>>62388676
AI training is a decent stopgap while you're looking for work. Mercor and Micro1 are best, Handshake's okay, Outlier sucks but doesn't pull shenanigans with your pay (I've heard that Alignerr does).

62402291
>>62400021
Dude im at like 8 months in total i just a took a month long break at the baggining of the year. I would have found a job by now.. I sidnt even disagree with anybody, they are just bullying me and breaking my spirit. Idk why they haven't fired me ye.t today is the last day of my PIP and I have heard nothing. I really just wanna ask my boss if they are gonna can me or not

62404970
>>62402291
Well, what happened?

62405224
>>62388694
>working myself 1000x harder
No, let's bring you back to reality. You're on /biz/ and the type of hard work you stated is reading white papers and then falling for the next make-it-big scam. You should have chased an honest life, but you're gambling and that does not pay.

62405704
>>62393491
Unemployment usually pays out. The only time it doesn’t is if you get fired for not showing up.

62406146
>>62404970

62407396
Reading this thread has really changed my opinion of the average American. I didn’t know you were all suffering like this. I finally understand Trump voters, I thought they were simply NPCs susceptible to marketing. They’re just stressed to the limit and want to blow the system up no matter what. Makes a bit more sense

I’m going to be nicer to everyone I meet and be more grateful for what I have.

62407517
>>62407396
Nah a lot of it is still NPCs susceptible to marketing. And racism. That said, even as a hardcore progressive, I'll be the first to tell you that Biden made things immeasurably worse, and waterboard me with milk a few times and I'll even admit that Obama didn't do as much for the American people as he could or should have. I genuinely wish he'd been the kind of full-blown socialist globalism clean energy sellout nigger anger monkey he got portrayed as, because then at least we'd have renewable energy and free college to go along with printing and spending trillions of dollars we're never going to get back (we used it on wars and PPP loans asset inflation instead).

I understand the desire to seriously reconsider some fundamental institutions.

62407855

62408366
>>62407709
This is related to the main reason underqualified men get hired when they do: it's when the boss sees them as a protege. This is why it's useless to apply places where affinity bias isn't working in your favor, and why it's way easier for tall men to get hired. Forget your CV, forget your technical interview; what matters is making the boss feel like they're looking at a younger version of themselves. They want to imbue with their knowledge, so that you can avoid the mistakes they made, and be a sort of redo of their early career.

>>62407517

Have fun.

62410448
>>62388676
OP, all your posts are about how horrible manual labor is and how you need to avoid it at all costs. You speak as if manual labor will destroy your ligaments and body but the truth is that sitting in a chair for 8 hours a day is actually literally way worse. You working at a construction site hauling concrete will definitely destroy your back, but you seem determined to avoid any job with the title of blue collar. There are some blue collar manufacturing jobs where your are simply babysitting some machine making gizmos and your only problem will be not going insane from how boring your shift is. I know a guy making $99k a year with a job like this. Unless you're physically disabled, change your outlook on this man please, you are very wrong.
